Description: Make every word earn its place. Remove redundant words and phrases.
Don't repeat information users can already see. Headers shouldn't restate what's obvious.
Code Example Bad:
Dashboard

This is your dashboard page where you can see your dashboard

Description: Button labels should be action-oriented and clear. 'Save' not 'Click to save'.
Use consistent language across the interface. Don't mix 'Save' and 'Store' for the same action.
Error messages should help users fix the problem. 'Please enter a valid email address' not 'Error'.
Error messages shouldn't blame the user. 'Unable to connect' not 'You entered the wrong password'.
Empty states should guide users toward action, not just say 'nothing here'.
Use encouraging tone in empty states. Help users get started.
Code Example Good: Get started by adding your first item

Description: Form labels should be clear and specific. 'Email address' not 'Email or username'.
Use microcopy to provide helpful hints without being verbose.
Code Example Bad: <input> or <input><small>This is your email address which we will use to contact you and we promise we will never share it with anyone else ever</small>
